title: The Assignment
paragraphs: Here's what I'm asking you to do.

Identify the person. Name the conversation. Figure out which persona is likely on the other side of the door. Plan your knock. Prepare for the foyer. Own your part in the kitchen. And earn your way to the lounge room.
Don't rush it. Don't skip a room. Don't punt on it because it's uncomfortable. The discomfort of the conversation is always, always less than the destruction of the silence.

Because here's what I've learned in forty years of ministry and thirty-nine years of marriage. The things that destroy families are not the things people said. They're the things people didn't say. The conversations they rehearsed but never had. The knocks they never knocked. The doors they walked past a thousand times and never opened.

Every one of these seven personas is made in the image of God. Every one of them is carrying something wounded underneath the behavior. The Wall is overwhelmed. The Deflector is terrified. The Volcano is wounded. The Minimizer is in denial. The Performer is exhausted. The Prosecutor is protecting their identity. The Ghost is grieving.

Your job is not to fix them. Your job is to see them. To knock in a way that makes them feel safe. To earn your way from the foyer to the kitchen to the lounge room. And to stay long enough for the soil to soften.
Proverbs 27:5–6 — "Better is open rebuke than hidden love. Faithful are the wounds of a friend; profuse are the kisses of an enemy."
The person who loves you enough to wound you with the truth is more faithful than the person who keeps the peace and watches you fall apart.
